subject: Biology
title: How Vaccines Work
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Vaccines Work.

hook.text: A vaccine teaches your immune system to recognize a pathogen—without you getting sick.

buildup.text: Vaccines contain weakened, killed, or fragment versions of a pathogen that can't cause disease.

discovery.text: Your immune system reacts, creates antibodies, and forms memory cells—just like a real infection.

twist.text: If the real pathogen ever appears, memory cells destroy it before symptoms start. You never know it happened.

climax.text: Vaccines have eradicated smallpox and nearly eliminated polio—saving hundreds of millions of lives.

punchline.text: A dress rehearsal that saves your life.

quiz.question: What do vaccines train the immune system to produce?
options: Memory cells and antibodies against a pathogen
